questions.question: Define Analysis of Covariance (ANCOVA) and explain its purpose in research.
keyPoints: ANCOVA is a statistical technique combining ANOVA and linear regression
It compares group means after adjusting for covariate effects
Purpose: reduce error variance, increase power, control confounding variables

questions.question: List and explain the main assumptions of ANCOVA.
keyPoints: Normality of residuals
Homogeneity of variances
Homogeneity of regression slopes
Linearity between covariate and dependent variable
Independent observations

questions.question: Explain the concept of adjusted means in ANCOVA with an example.
keyPoints: Adjusted means are group means after removing covariate effects
They represent what means would be if all groups had same covariate value
Example: comparing test scores after adjusting for study hours

questions.question: What is the homogeneity of regression slopes assumption? How is it tested?
keyPoints: Regression slope should be same across all groups
Tested by including interaction term (Group × Covariate)
If interaction is significant, assumption is violated

questions.question: How does including a covariate improve the statistical power of an experiment?
keyPoints: Covariate explains some variance in dependent variable
Reduces error variance (SSW)
With smaller error, treatment effects are easier to detect
Requires smaller sample size for same power

questions.question: Differentiate between One-Way ANCOVA and Two-Way ANCOVA.
keyPoints: One-Way: one factor and one or more covariates
Two-Way: two factors and covariates
One-Way tests main effect only
Two-Way tests main effects and interaction

questions.question: What is Eta-squared (η²) in ANCOVA and how is it interpreted?
keyPoints: η² is the effect size measure
Represents proportion of variance explained by treatment after covariate control
Values: small (0.01), medium (0.06), large (0.14)

questions.question: Explain how the Total Sum of Squares (SST) is partitioned in ANCOVA. Derive the formula and explain each component with its significance.
keyPoints: SST = SSB + SSW + SSC + SS(B×C) where SSC is covariate and B×C is interaction
SSB: variability due to treatment between groups
SSW: within-group variability (error)
SSC: variability explained by covariate
SS(B×C): interaction between treatment and covariate
F = MSB/MSW tests significance after adjustment

questions.question: A researcher wants to compare the performance of three sorting algorithms (QuickSort, MergeSort, HeapSort) on execution time. However, input array size affects execution time. How would ANCOVA be applied? Define variables, state assumptions, and explain the interpretation.
keyPoints: Dependent variable: execution time
Factor: sorting algorithm (3 levels)
Covariate: input array size
Assumptions: normality, homogeneity of slopes, linearity, homogeneity of variances
Interpretation: Compare adjusted mean execution times controlling for array size
Reduced error variance due to covariate increases power

questions.question: What are the consequences of violating the homogeneity of regression slopes assumption in ANCOVA? How would you address this violation?
keyPoints: Violation means covariate effect differs across groups
Interaction term becomes significant
ANCOVA results may be invalid
Solutions: Include interaction term in model, Use separate slopes model, Transform variables, Use non-parametric alternatives

questions.question: Derive the F-statistic formula for testing the significance of treatment effect in ANCOVA and explain its interpretation.
keyPoints: F = MSB(adjusted) / MSW(adjusted)
MSB = SSB(df between) adjusted for covariate
MSW = SSW(df within) after removing covariate effect
Interpretation: If F is significant, adjusted group means differ significantly
Degrees of freedom: df between = (k-1), df within = (N-k-1) where k=groups
